sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Real Betis are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 4 matches with notable wins like a 3-0 victory over Mallorca and a 2-0 win against Olympique Lyonnais. Girona FC, struggling at 18th in the league, have had mixed results in their last 5 games, managing only 1 win against Deportivo Alavés (1-0) and suffering losses to stronger teams like Barcelona (2-1).

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Real Betis have the upper hand, securing a convincing 3-1 away win against Girona FC in April 2025, while the earlier match in August 2024 ended in a 1-1 draw. Historical trends show both teams often score, with 7 out of 7 recent head-to-heads featuring goals from both sides.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Betis, sitting 5th with 20 points, have a solid offensive output (19 goals, 5th in the league) and a strong defense (13 conceded, 5th least). Girona FC, with only 11 goals scored and 24 conceded, struggle defensively and lack consistency in attack, ranking near the bottom of the table.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: For Real Betis, players like Cucho Hernández (5 goals) and in-form Antony (3 goals in last 5 matches) pose significant threats. Girona’s Cristhian Stuani has been their standout, scoring 4 goals in recent matches, but the team misses key players like Daley Blind, which could hurt their structure.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: With Sofascore predicting a 69% chance of a home win, Real Betis are clear favorites at Estadio de la Cartuja. Girona’s defensive frailties and poor away form suggest they’ll struggle, while Betis’ balanced attack and home advantage should see them dominate.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Girona FC leads Real Betis with a narrow 1-0 scoreline at Estadio de la Cartuja in Seville. The first half saw Girona capitalize on a well-taken opportunity through Vladyslav Vanat in the 20th minute. Real Betis, despite having slightly more possession, struggled to convert their chances into goals. Both teams showed moments of promise, but Girona's clinical finishing made the difference.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis showed decent control in the first half, managing to dominate possession marginally. They had several entries into the final third and penalty area, indicating an intent to attack, but lacked the cutting edge in front of goal. Their expected goals suggest they had opportunities, yet failed to create big chances. On the other hand, Girona FC was more efficient, creating big chances and converting one to take the lead. Their expected goals reflect a stronger attacking output despite having slightly less possession. Defensively, Girona also looked more composed, winning a higher percentage of aerial duels.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half began with Real Betis pushing forward aggressively, showing strong attacking intent in the opening minutes. Their momentum was consistent for a significant period, with multiple spells of sustained pressure in the opposition half. However, around the time of Girona's goal, there was a noticeable shift as Girona took control with a series of threatening attacks. They maintained this momentum for several minutes post-goal, keeping Betis on the back foot. Towards the latter stages of the half, Betis regained some ground with renewed attacking vigor, while Girona seemed content to protect their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key player contributions
halftimeSections.text: For Girona FC, Vladyslav Vanat was the standout performer, scoring the crucial goal and posing a constant threat upfront. Bryan Gil also played a pivotal role with an assist and involvement in creating dangerous opportunities. On the Real Betis side, Pablo Fornals showed creativity with key passes, attempting to unlock Girona's defense. Marc Roca was solid in midfield, contributing both offensively and defensively. However, Betis will need more from their forwards like Antony and Cucho Hernández to turn their chances into goals in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis seems to be focusing on building play through the midfield, aiming to penetrate Girona’s defense with passes into the final third. However, their inability to create clear-cut chances suggests a need for quicker transitions or more direct play in the second half. Girona, meanwhile, has adopted a balanced approach, defending compactly while looking to exploit spaces on the counter. Their success in creating big chances indicates they are dangerous when given room to attack, and Betis must be wary of this threat.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see Real Betis push harder for an equalizer, potentially leaving gaps at the back for Girona to exploit. Betis will need to be more clinical and may look to increase the tempo of their attacks. Girona, with the lead, might focus on maintaining a solid defensive structure while seeking opportunities to strike on the break. Given the first-half stats, both teams have the potential to score, but Girona’s efficiency could give them an edge if Betis overcommits.
